2.text_description:
The original theatrical release US poster for The Dark Knight (2008) depicts a dark urban night scene focused on the Joker's presence.
At the center of the poster is a tall skyscraper. In the middle of the building's facade, a fiery pattern has been burned into the windows, forming the shape of the Batman logo. The edges of the windows surrounding this burn pattern glow with orange flames, while smoke rises upward from the burning sections. The rest of the building's windows are dark.
In the lower portion of the poster, the Joker is shown from behind, standing in the street and facing the burning building. He is holding a gun in his right hand, which hangs down at his side. His body is centered, with his legs slightly apart, and his head is turned slightly to the side, making his green hair partially visible. He wears his long purple coat, which extends almost to his knees.
Near the top of the poster, in large white capital letters, is the tagline: "WELCOME TO A WORLD WITHOUT RULES."
Below the burning Batman logo, near the bottom of the poster, is the title: "THE DARK KNIGHT." The title is written in bold white letters, with the Batman emblem placed between the words "DARK" and "KNIGHT."
Beneath the title, the release date is shown: "SUMMER 2008."
At the very bottom of the poster is a block of billing text listing the main cast, director, producers, and production companies. The Warner Bros. and DC Comics logos also appear in this section.

3.text_description:
The original theatrical release U.S. poster for The Matrix (1999) shows the four main characters standing together in front of a textured, blue-gray, industrial wall with streaks resembling cascading computer code.

Foreground characters (centered group):

Neo (Keanu Reeves) stands in the middle, slightly ahead of the others. He wears a long black trench coat, black shirt, pants, and boots, with small oval black sunglasses. His arms are relaxed by his sides.

Morpheus (Laurence Fishburne) is to Neo's left. He wears a long purple snakeskin-patterned coat, dark pants, black boots, and round mirrored sunglasses. He holds a handgun in his right hand pointed downward.

Trinity (Carrie-Anne Moss) is to Neo's right. She wears a tight black leather sleeveless top, black pants, and boots, with small black sunglasses. She holds a pistol in her right hand at her side.

Cypher (Joe Pantoliano) stands slightly behind, to Morpheus's left. He wears a dark jacket, dark shirt, pants, and round sunglasses. He holds a gun in his right hand angled downward.

Background:
The wall behind them is covered with a faint vertical pattern of digital code-like streaks. The surface looks metallic and industrial, with a cold tone.

Title and text:

The word "MATRIX" appears at the top, centered, in a distinct blue font with digital distortions in the letters.

The cast names — "KEANU REEVES LAURENCE FISHBURNE CARRIE-ANNE MOSS HUGO WEAVING" — are positioned above the title, in smaller white capital letters.

At the bottom are the credits block and film rating, aligned across the width of the poster, in standard white lettering.

No tagline is present on this original U.S. theatrical version.

7.text_description:
The original U.S. theatrical release poster of Inception (2010) shows a city street scene viewed from ground level.
Foreground / Characters: Six main characters are standing in the middle of the street, all facing forward toward the viewer but angled slightly differently. From left to right:
A man in a dark suit holding a gun at his side (Arthur, played by Joseph Gordon-Levitt).
A man in a long coat holding a gun (Eames, played by Tom Hardy).
In the center, a man in a dark suit with a tie (Dom Cobb, played by Leonardo DiCaprio). He is the most prominent figure.
To his right, a man in a suit with his hands at his sides (Saito, played by Ken Watanabe).
A woman in a blazer and pants (Ariadne, played by Ellen Page).
On the far right, another man in a suit (Robert Fischer, played by Cillian Murphy).
Background: The cityscape shows tall modern buildings on both sides of the street. The buildings are unnaturally tilted inward at the top, creating a distorted perspective as if the city is bending. Some of the windows and glass surfaces reflect light. The street is partly flooded with water rising above curb level, creating the impression that the city is partially submerged.
Street Details: The pavement has cracks, and water is pooled around the characters' feet. There are no visible cars or people besides the main characters.
Text:
At the top of the poster, in small white capital letters, is the tagline: "FROM THE DIRECTOR OF THE DARK KNIGHT."
Near the bottom, the title "INCEPTION" is printed in large red block letters.
Beneath the title, the release information is given in smaller white text: "JULY 16."
The production credits appear in small type at the very bottom edge of the poster, including names of cast, crew, and production companies.
Other elements: No review quotes, awards, or external symbols are present.

9.poster_name: Star Wars: Episode IV - A New Hope (1977)
9.text_description:
Here's a detailed and accurate description of the original U.S. theatrical release poster of Star Wars: Episode IV – A New Hope (1977) designed by Tom Jung:
Main central figures:
At the bottom center, Luke Skywalker is shown standing with his legs apart. He holds a lightsaber raised straight above his head with both hands. The lightsaber extends upward in a vertical line, glowing brightly.
To his left, Princess Leia stands slightly in front of him. She is leaning forward with one knee bent on a platform and her other leg extended back. She holds a blaster in her right hand, aimed outward.
Background:
Behind the two central figures, a large, semi-transparent image of Darth Vader's helmeted head looms over them, dominating the top portion of the poster.
Starships are visible in the background: X-wings are flying in formation, some engaged in battle with TIE fighters.
The Death Star is shown in the upper right area of the poster.
Laser blasts and explosions are scattered across the space battle scene.
Foreground platform:
Luke and Leia are standing on a small rocky or pedestal-like surface, giving them elevation above the lower part of the poster.
Droids:
At the lower left corner, R2-D2 and C-3PO are positioned together, looking outward toward the viewer.
Text elements:
At the top, in large letters, is the film's title: "STAR WARS" written in bold block-style lettering.
At the bottom portion of the poster, there is a credit block with cast and crew names, including Mark Hamill, Harrison Ford, Carrie Fisher, Peter Cushing, Alec Guinness, as well as production credits (Written and Directed by George Lucas, etc.).
The tagline appears at the top of the poster above the title: "A long time ago in a galaxy far, far away…"

10.poster_name: Star Wars: Episode V - The Empire Strikes Back (1980)
10.text_description:
The original theatrical release U.S. poster for Star Wars: Episode V – The Empire Strikes Back (1980), designed by Roger Kastel, presents the following details:
Central figures: At the center, Han Solo and Princess Leia are prominently depicted. Han is holding Leia in his arms in a dramatic, romantic embrace, his head lowered toward her as if about to kiss. Leia leans back slightly in his arms, facing upward.
Background imagery:
Behind Han and Leia, the head and upper body of Darth Vader dominate the composition. Vader's helmeted head looms large, occupying a significant portion of the top background.
To the right side of Vader, the Millennium Falcon is shown flying at an angle, with motion indicated by light trails behind it.
In the lower part of the poster, toward the bottom center, a group of Rebel soldiers on tauntauns is depicted, moving across an icy, snowy plain that represents Hoth. AT-AT walkers are also shown in the distance, advancing across the same snowy battlefield.
X-wing fighters and TIE fighters are scattered in the sky area, shown in mid-battle.
Boba Fett appears in the lower right, holding his weapon.
Lando Calrissian and C-3PO are depicted in smaller scale among the supporting characters.
Title text: The title "The Empire Strikes Back" is prominently displayed in bold lettering, placed toward the bottom portion of the poster. Above it, in smaller print, is the line "Star Wars" (styled consistently with the franchise branding).
Billing block: Below the title, a standard billing block is included, with cast and crew credits in white text.
Tagline: Near the top of the poster is the tagline in white lettering: "The Star Wars saga continues."
Everything present in the composition works around Vader's looming head, Han and Leia's embrace at the center, the battle and supporting characters filling the lower half, and the text elements framing it at the top and bottom.

14.poster_name: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)
14.text_description:
The original theatrical release US poster for Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002) is a character ensemble design that depicts the main cast and elements from the film:
At the center foreground, Harry Potter is shown holding a lit wand in his right hand, pointed forward. He is dressed in his Hogwarts uniform (robe, white shirt, striped tie), and he wears his round glasses.
Just behind and slightly to Harry's right, Hermione Granger is visible. She also wears her Hogwarts uniform and robe, and she has a determined expression.
To Harry's left, Ron Weasley appears, also in his school robes. He looks alert and slightly tense.
In the background, upper left, Professor Dumbledore's face is seen, looking down over the group.
On the upper right side, Professor Snape is shown in a dark robe, looking serious.
Below Snape, Draco Malfoy is visible, also in Hogwarts robes, with his distinctive blond hair.
In the far background behind Harry, faintly lit, the giant figure of Dobby the house-elf can be seen.
At the bottom of the poster, Fawkes the phoenix is present, wings slightly spread, its red and gold plumage visible.
The environment surrounding the characters suggests the interior of Hogwarts, with stone arches and a sense of depth leading into a dark corridor.
Text elements:
At the very top, the line reads: "Something evil has returned to Hogwarts."
Near the bottom, the film's title "Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets" is displayed prominently in the familiar Harry Potter logo font.
Beneath the title, the release date is shown: "November 15."
At the bottom edge in smaller text are the production credits, listing Warner Bros., Heyday Films, the director, producers, and other crew members.

15.text_description:
The original theatrical release US poster of Back to the Future (1985), designed by Drew Struzan, shows a single central image of Marty McFly standing beside the DeLorean time machine.
Marty is positioned in the foreground, slightly to the right, with his body angled toward the left. He is lifting his left leg as if stepping out of the car and has his left foot on the ground. His right hand is raised to his forehead, pushing up his sunglasses to look at his wristwatch on his left wrist. His head is turned slightly to the left, and he is looking at the watch. He is wearing a red T-shirt, a denim jacket, an orange down vest, jeans, and sneakers.
Behind him is the open gull-wing driver's door of the DeLorean, raised upward. The car is facing left at an angle, with its headlights on and brightly shining. Its rear tires are not visible, but flames trail out from the bottom of the car, extending forward and outward in parallel lines from underneath it, creating two fiery tracks across the ground in front of Marty.
The background is dark but has a large area of glowing light and sky behind Marty and the car, shifting from bright white/yellow near the car and Marty to darker blue and black further out.
At the top of the poster, there is the text "Steven Spielberg Presents" followed by the large movie title Back to the Future. The title is angled, with "Back to the" in smaller type above the large word "Future," and the text has a gradient color.
Below the main image, along the bottom, is the billing block with cast and crew credits in white text. Above the billing block is the movie's tagline: He was never in time for his classes… He wasn't in time for his dinner… Then one day… he wasn't in his time at all.
The poster overall contains only Marty, the DeLorean with its door open and headlights shining, the fiery tracks beneath it, the tagline, the title, and the credits.

17.poster_name: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003)
17.text_description:
The original theatrical release U.S. poster for The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003) is a character-collage style composition featuring multiple main figures from the film, layered prominently in the frame, with the title and credits placed at the bottom.
At the center foreground, Aragorn is the most dominant figure. He is shown holding a sword with both hands, angled diagonally upward, his body turned slightly while facing forward. His image is the largest on the poster.
Directly behind and above Aragorn, Gandalf appears, holding his staff upright in one hand. His face is clearly visible, and his white robes and hair distinguish him.
To Aragorn's right side (viewer's left), Frodo is shown holding the One Ring on a chain, his expression focused. Slightly below Frodo, Sam is also depicted, close to him, facing forward.
On Aragorn's opposite side (viewer's right), Legolas is included, drawn with his bow at the ready. Below Legolas, Arwen appears, her head slightly turned to the side.
Other characters are also arranged in the composition: Gimli is visible near Legolas, and Éowyn appears in armor with her helmet off. Gollum is included near the lower portion, crouched and staring upward.
In the distant background, above and around the central figures, scenes from the film are integrated: a large image of Minas Tirith, the White City, is visible, along with a wide view of a battle taking place, including cavalry and soldiers in combat. The imagery suggests a sweeping battlefield but does not focus on individual unnamed soldiers.
At the very bottom, the film's title The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King is printed in a large gold font, with the subtitle "The Return of the King" smaller than the main series title. Beneath the title, the full billing block of credits is included, along with the New Line Cinema logo. The release date, "December 17," is also shown.

19.poster_name: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
19.text_description:
The original theatrical release US poster for The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) features a vertical composition with multiple characters from the film arranged in layers, filling almost the entire space.
At the top left, Gandalf the Grey is shown prominently, his face large and looking downward to the right, holding his staff.
Slightly lower and to the right of Gandalf, Aragorn appears, facing forward, holding a sword angled downward.
Frodo Baggins is positioned near the center foreground, the most prominent of the hobbits. He is shown holding the One Ring in his open palm, glowing faintly. His expression is serious.
Directly beneath Frodo and extending across the lower half are the other hobbits: Samwise Gamgee, Merry, and Pippin, grouped together. They are smaller in scale compared to Frodo.
To the right side, Legolas appears with his bow drawn.
Gimli is shown below Legolas, with his axe visible.
Further in the background, Arwen appears in flowing clothing, facing forward.
Behind and above her, Saruman is depicted with a stern expression, also large in scale.
In the far background, faint imagery of the Ringwraiths on horseback can be seen, riding in formation.
At the bottom portion of the poster, the title "The Lord of the Rings" is displayed in large golden text, with the subtitle "The Fellowship of the Ring" underneath in smaller type.
Beneath the title is the standard credit block with production details (director, producers, cast, etc.).
At the very bottom, the New Line Cinema logo is present, along with the website URL www.lordoftherings.net and the release date December 19, 2001.
